mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of AI models while optimizing performance through intelligent load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate hardware resources and the absence of dependencies on other systems like Istio or Knative.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://github.com/substratusai/kubeai/releases/download/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1

```

> This command adds the KubeAI repository for Helm.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_kubeai_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for KubeAI.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeAI operator into the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> This command installs KubeAI in the kubeai namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to deploy a machine learning model.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: KubeAI
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  model: 'your-model-name'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Replace 'your-model-name' with the actual model you want to deploy.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: KubeAI
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  model: 'your-model-name'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the KubeAI deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the KubeAI deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai

```

> This command lists the KubeAI resources in the kubeai namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the KubeAI operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all models and configurations associated with KubeAI.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> This command uninstalls the KubeAI Helm release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your existing configurations. This step involves upgrading the KubeAI operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai

```

> Backup existing KubeAI configurations.
> This command upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the KubeAI resources are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeAI not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI operator may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure KubeAI is installed and check the namespace with 'kubectl get namespaces'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create KubeAI resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_kubeai_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model specified in the Custom Resource may not be available or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the model name and configuration in your Custom Resource and correct it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources (CPU, memory) allocated.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements: 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI resources listed with 'kubectl get kubeai -n kubeai', and the status should indicate they are running without errors.

spec:

  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
